title: SPY ORB backtests are almost always wrong — here's why
author: FantasticShine4012
selftext:
Opening Range Breakout on SPY looks amazing on paper. Then you trade it live and it underperforms. I went looking for why.



The issue is almost always in the data:



Pre-market data handling — Most retail platforms define "open" differently. Some include pre-market candles in the first bar, some don't. Your ORB range changes completely.



Adjusted vs unadjusted closes — Dividend adjustments backfill the entire price history. An unadjusted $420 close becomes $418.73 retroactively. Your entries and SL levels are now phantom prices.



Volume gaps at open — The 9:30 bar on most CSV exports is often incomplete (partial minute). Any volume-based filter fails silently.



I replicated a well-known ORB setup on 3 different data sources and got 3 completely different results. Same code, same parameters.



Anyone else benchmarked this? What data source are you using for US equities intraday that you actually trust?
